2606.27700.title: Joint Transcription and Decryption of Images of Encrypted Handwritten Documents: A Comparison with the Traditional Pipeline
2606.27700.paper_summary: Historical encrypted manuscripts present a challenging problem at the intersection of cryptology, linguistics, paleography, and computer vision. Current automatic decipherment approaches usually rely on a two-stage pipeline: transcription of cipher symbols from manuscript images, followed by decryption into plaintext. However, this design is sensitive to transcription errors, which propagate to the final output. We present Direct Image Decryption, an end-to-end approach that directly maps encrypted manuscript images to plaintext, bypassing the intermediate transcription stage. Using the Copiale cipher as a case study, we build a synthetic data generation pipeline to create large-scale cipher-like training data and compare the traditional pipeline with the proposed joint architecture. Results show that joint image-to-plaintext modeling is a promising alternative to traditional transcription-based pipelines.
2606.27700.paper_summary_zh: 歷史加密手稿在密碼學、語言學、古文字學和計算機視覺的交匯處提出了一個挑戰性的問題。<lb/>當前的自動解碼方法通常依賴於兩階段的流程：首先從手稿圖像中轉錄密碼符號，然後將其解密為明文。<lb/>然而，這種設計對轉錄錯誤非常敏感，這些錯誤會傳播到最終輸出。<lb/>我們提出了直接圖像解密，這是一種端到端的方法，直接將加密的手稿圖像映射到明文，繞過中間的轉錄階段。<lb/>以Copiale密碼為案例研究，我們建立了一個合成數據生成管道，以創建大規模的類密碼訓練數據，並將傳統管道與所提出的聯合架構進行比較。<lb/>結果顯示，聯合圖像到明文建模是傳統基於轉錄的管道的一個有前景的替代方案。
2606.27700.author: Marino Oliveros-Blanco et.al.
2606.27700.authors: Marino Oliveros-Blanco, Lei Kang, Alicia Fornés, Beáta Megyesi
